description: Language Pre-Test, Tutorial & Onboarding System - in-game Arrival Encounter assessment with guide NPC, 4-phase scoring (conversation/listening/writing/visual), CEFR mapping, generic skill tree/onboarding frameworks, admin + player analytics, shared/ directory reorganization

userStories.title: Generic skill tree framework
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a generic skill tree abstraction so that language learning, RPG, and other game genres can all use parameterized skill trees without duplicating logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/skill-tree/generic-skill-tree.ts with SkillNode<TConditionType>, SkillTier<TConditionType>, SkillTreeState<TConditionType>, and updateSkillProgress<TConditionType>(state, statsMap) where TConditionType is a string union
The generic updateSkillProgress accepts a Record<TConditionType, number> stats map and evaluates thresholds — same logic as existing language-skill-tree.ts lines 254-279 but parameterized

Create shared/skill-tree/language-skill-tree.ts that defines LanguageSkillConditionType = 'words_learned' | 'words_mastered' | 'conversations' | 'grammar_patterns' | 'target_language_pct' | 'fluency' | 'sustained_turns' | 'quest_count' and re-exports LANGUAGE_SKILL_TIERS (the existing 5-tier SKILL_TIERS data)
Create shared/skill-tree/index.ts barrel file exporting both generic and language-specific types
Update existing shared/language-skill-tree.ts to re-export from shared/skill-tree/language-skill-tree.ts for backward compatibility
All existing imports of language-skill-tree continue to work
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing language-skill-tree.ts has SkillNode, SkillCondition, SkillTier, SKILL_TIERS, SkillTreeState, createDefaultSkillTreeState(), and updateSkillProgress(). Extract the generic parts, keeping language-specific condition types and tier data separate.

userStories.title: Create shared/language/ directory and move language files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want language-related shared files organized in shared/language/ so the codebase is easier to navigate.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/language/ directory
Move shared/language-progress.ts to shared/language/progress.ts
Move shared/language-gamification.ts to shared/language/gamification.ts
Move shared/language-quest-templates.ts to shared/language/quest-templates.ts
Move shared/language-utils.ts to shared/language/utils.ts
Move shared/language-vocabulary-corpus.ts to shared/language/vocabulary-corpus.ts
Move shared/language.ts to shared/language/types.ts
Move shared/pronunciation-scoring.ts to shared/language/pronunciation-scoring.ts
Move shared/character-language-profile.ts to shared/language/character-profile.ts
Move shared/bilingual-name-generation.ts to shared/language/bilingual-names.ts
Create shared/language/index.ts barrel file that re-exports all public types and functions from all moved files
Fix any internal import paths between the moved files (they may import from each other)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git mv for moves so git tracks the rename. language-utils.ts imports from language.ts (now types.ts) and quest-types/language-learning.ts — update those internal paths. The barrel index.ts should export * from each file.

userStories.title: Backward-compatible re-export files for moved language files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the old import paths to continue working so existing code across client and server does not break.
acceptanceCriteria: Create re-export file at shared/language-progress.ts: export * from './language/progress'
Create re-export file at shared/language-gamification.ts: export * from './language/gamification'
Create re-export file at shared/language-quest-templates.ts: export * from './language/quest-templates'
Create re-export file at shared/language-utils.ts: export * from './language/utils'
Create re-export file at shared/language-vocabulary-corpus.ts: export * from './language/vocabulary-corpus'
Create re-export file at shared/language.ts: export * from './language/types'
Create re-export file at shared/pronunciation-scoring.ts: export * from './language/pronunciation-scoring'
Create re-export file at shared/character-language-profile.ts: export * from './language/character-profile'
Create re-export file at shared/bilingual-name-generation.ts: export * from './language/bilingual-names'
All existing imports across client/ and server/ resolve without changes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Each re-export file is a single line: export * from './language/[new-name]'. This ensures zero breakage. The old files were moved in US-002, so these are new files at the old paths.

userStories.title: Assessment type definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared assessment type definitions so that client, server, and analytics code all use the same data structures.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/assessment/ directory

Create shared/assessment/assessment-types.ts with interfaces: AssessmentDefinition (id, name, phases: AssessmentPhase[], totalMaxScore, cefrMapping?), AssessmentPhase (id, name, type: 'conversational' | 'listening' | 'writing' | 'visual' | 'custom', tasks: AssessmentTask[], maxScore, durationMinutes), AssessmentTask (id, type, prompt, scoringDimensions: ScoringDimension[], maxScore, config?), ScoringDimension (id, name, scale: [number, number], weight), AssessmentSession (id, definitionId, playerId, worldId, playthroughId, assessmentType: 'pre' | 'post' | 'periodic', status: 'in_progress' | 'completed' | 'abandoned', startedAt, completedAt, phaseResults: PhaseResult[], totalScore, maxScore, cefrLevel?, metadata?), PhaseResult (phaseId, taskResults: TaskResult[], score, maxScore, automatedMetrics?), TaskResult (taskId, responses: any, dimensionScores: Record<string, number>, rawScore), AutomatedMetrics (speechRateWPM?, lexicalDiversityTTR?, meanLengthOfUtterance?, responseLatencyMs?, highestSustainedTier?, repairRequests?, grammarErrorRate?, codeSwitchingInstances?), RecordingReference (id, phaseId, taskId?, audioUrl?, transcriptText?, timestamp, durationMs?)
Create shared/assessment/index.ts barrel file exporting all types
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are pure type definitions — no logic. All fields with ? are optional. The types must cover the full assessment lifecycle from creation to completion.

userStories.title: CEFR score mapping
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that maps raw assessment scores to CEFR levels so the system can classify player proficiency.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/assessment/cefr-mapping.ts with: CEFRLevel type ('A1' | 'A2' | 'B1' | 'B1+' | 'B2'), mapScoreToCEFR(totalScore, maxScore) function using thresholds: A1 (0-19%), A2 (20-39%), B1 (40-60%), B1+ (61-79%), B2 (80-100%)
Add generic mapScoreToLevel<T>(score, maxScore, levelMap: Array<{threshold: number, level: T}>): T for non-CEFR use cases
Add getCEFRDescription(level: CEFRLevel): string returning human-readable descriptions (e.g. 'A1 - Beginner: Can understand and use familiar everyday expressions')
Export from shared/assessment/index.ts barrel
Pure functions, unit-testable
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Score thresholds as percentages allow use with different maxScore values (53 for full assessment, 25 for periodic). The generic mapScoreToLevel enables future reuse for non-language level systems.

userStories.title: Arrival Encounter pre-test definition
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Arrival Encounter pre-test fully defined as data so the assessment engine can execute it phase by phase.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/assessment/arrival-encounter.ts exporting ARRIVAL_ENCOUNTER: AssessmentDefinition

Phase 1 (conversational, 8-10 min, maxScore 25): 5 scoring dimensions (oralFluency, pronunciation, grammarAccuracy, vocabularyRange, pragmaticCompetence) each scale [1,5] weight 1.0; 3 tier definitions with specific NPC prompts — Tier 1 (A1-A2): greeting/basic info/preferences, Tier 2 (A2-B1): giving info/describing/narration, Tier 3 (B1-B2): opinions/hypotheticals/comparisons

Phase 2 (listening, 3-4 min, maxScore 7): Task 2A Following Directions (maxScore 4, prompt for multi-step navigation), Task 2B Information Extraction (maxScore 3, prompt for 3-option selection with 3 questions)

Phase 3 (writing, 3-4 min, maxScore 11): Task 3A Form Completion (maxScore 5, fields: nationality/reason/length/requests), Task 3B Brief Message (maxScore 6, scoring: taskCompletion 0-2, vocabulary 0-2, grammar 0-2)
Phase 4 (visual, 2-3 min, maxScore 10): Task 4A Sign Reading (maxScore 5, 5 signs with 3-option multiple choice), Task 4B Object Identification (maxScore 5, 5 objects)
Total maxScore: 53
Tier escalation rules defined as data: advanceAfterSuccessfulExchanges (3), dropBackOnStruggles (true), maxTier (3)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a data definition file, not logic. The prompts should be template strings with {{targetLanguage}} and {{cityName}} placeholders. Based on dissertation at /dissertation/evaluation/comprehensive/main-study/01-pre-test.tex.

userStories.title: Departure Encounter post-test definition
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the parallel Departure Encounter post-test with different scenarios but equivalent structure and scoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/assessment/departure-encounter.ts exporting DEPARTURE_ENCOUNTER: AssessmentDefinition

Phase 1: Farewell conversation — Tier 1: farewell/reflection/preferences, Tier 2: recounting/recommendations/problem-solving, Tier 3: comparison/opinion/hypothetical. Same 5 dimensions, same scoring scale
Phase 2: Task 2A narrative comprehension (maxScore 3), Task 2B transactional listening (maxScore 4). Total 7
Phase 3: Task 3A departure form (maxScore 5), Task 3B review/postcard message (maxScore 6). Total 11
Phase 4: Task 4A departure signs (maxScore 5), Task 4B departure objects (maxScore 5). Total 10
Same total maxScore: 53. Same structure as Arrival Encounter but different prompts/scenarios
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Based on dissertation at /dissertation/evaluation/comprehensive/main-study/02-post-test.tex. Different content from arrival but parallel structure for valid pre-post comparison.

userStories.title: Generic onboarding framework types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a genre-agnostic onboarding framework so that language learning, RPG, FPS, and other genres can define their own intro/tutorial sequences.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/onboarding/ directory

Create shared/onboarding/onboarding-types.ts with: OnboardingDefinition (id, name, genre, steps: OnboardingStep[], assessmentId?), OnboardingStep (id, type: 'narrative' | 'movement_tutorial' | 'interaction_tutorial' | 'assessment_phase' | 'ui_tutorial' | 'combat_tutorial' | 'custom', config: Record<string, any>, completionCriteria: string, optional: boolean, durationEstimate?: number), OnboardingState (definitionId, currentStepIndex, completed, stepStates: Record<string, StepState>, startedAt?, completedAt?), StepState (stepId, status: 'pending' | 'active' | 'completed' | 'skipped', data?: any)
Create shared/onboarding/index.ts barrel file
Types are genre-agnostic — no language-learning specifics in the generic types
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep types minimal and extensible. The config Record<string, any> allows genre-specific step configuration without polluting the generic interface.

userStories.title: Language learning onboarding definition
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the language-learning onboarding sequence defined as data, weaving assessment phases with tutorial steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/onboarding/language-onboarding.ts exporting LANGUAGE_LEARNING_ONBOARDING: OnboardingDefinition

10 steps defined: (1) narrative - guide NPC approaches/introduces world, (2) movement_tutorial - WASD/camera, (3) assessment_phase - Phase 1 conversational, (4) movement_tutorial - walk to destination (doubles as Phase 2A), (5) assessment_phase - Phase 2B info extraction, (6) interaction_tutorial - interact with objects, (7) assessment_phase - Phase 3 writing, (8) ui_tutorial - vocab/quest/skill panels, (9) assessment_phase - Phase 4 visual, (10) narrative - guide summarizes, gives first quest
Each assessment_phase step references the corresponding phase/task ID from ARRIVAL_ENCOUNTER
Genre set to 'language-learning'
assessmentId references 'arrival-encounter'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a data definition. Import AssessmentPhase IDs from arrival-encounter.ts to reference them. Steps 3-9 are the assessment phases disguised as gameplay.

userStories.title: Assessment MongoDB schema and basic CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I need assessment sessions persisted in MongoDB with basic create/read/update operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Add AssessmentSessionSchema to server/db/mongo-storage.ts matching the AssessmentSession type from shared/assessment/assessment-types.ts, plus recordings: [RecordingReference] array
Add compound index on { playerId: 1, worldId: 1, assessmentType: 1 }

Add IStorage interface methods and MongoStorage implementations: createAssessmentSession(session) returns created session, getAssessmentSession(id) returns session or undefined, updateAssessmentPhaseResult(sessionId, phaseResult) appends/updates a phase result in the phaseResults array, addAssessmentRecording(sessionId, recording) pushes to recordings array
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The IStorage interface is in server/db/storage.ts. Add the new methods there, then implement in MongoStorage. Use mongoose's $push for array appends. Schema should use Schema.Types.Mixed for flexible nested objects like PhaseResult and AutomatedMetrics.

userStories.title: Assessment MongoDB completion and query methods
userStories.description: As a developer, I need assessment session completion, player queries, and admin aggregation methods in MongoDB.
acceptanceCriteria: Add IStorage + MongoStorage method: completeAssessmentSession(sessionId, totalScore, cefrLevel) — sets status to 'completed', completedAt to now, totalScore and cefrLevel
Add IStorage + MongoStorage method: getPlayerAssessments(playerId, worldId?) — returns all sessions for a player, optionally filtered by worldId, sorted by startedAt desc

Add IStorage + MongoStorage method: getWorldAssessmentSummary(worldId) — MongoDB aggregation returning: totalCount, meanScore, scoreDistribution (histogram buckets), cefrDistribution (count per level), meanAutomatedMetrics (averages of each metric across all completed sessions)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: getWorldAssessmentSummary uses MongoDB aggregation pipeline with $group, $avg, $bucket for score distribution. Only aggregate completed sessions.

userStories.title: Assessment API routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need REST API endpoints for assessment lifecycle management.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/assessment-routes.ts with a createAssessmentRoutes(storage) function that returns an Express Router
POST /api/assessments — creates session (body: playerId, worldId, playthroughId, definitionId, assessmentType). Validates required fields. Returns 201 with session
GET /api/assessments/:sessionId — returns session with all results. 404 if not found
PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/phases/:phaseId — submits phase result (body: PhaseResult). 404 if session not found
PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/recordings — adds recording reference (body: RecordingReference). 404 if not found
PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/complete — calls completeAssessmentSession with computed total score and mapScoreToCEFR result. Returns updated session
GET /api/assessments/player/:playerId — lists assessments (query: worldId?, assessmentType?)
GET /api/assessments/world/:worldId/summary — returns aggregation from getWorldAssessmentSummary
GET /api/assessments/:sessionId/recordings — returns recordings array for session
Register routes in server/routes.ts or server/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the same pattern as createTelemetryRoutes() in server/routes/telemetry-routes.ts for dependency injection of storage. Import mapScoreToCEFR from shared/assessment/cefr-mapping.

userStories.title: GameEventBus assessment events
userStories.description: As a developer, I need assessment-related events in the GameEventBus so all game systems can react to assessment lifecycle changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 8 new event types to the GameEvent discriminated union in client/src/components/3DGame/GameEventBus.ts: assessment_started (sessionId, assessmentType, definitionId), assessment_phase_started (sessionId, phaseId, phaseType), assessment_phase_completed (sessionId, phaseId, score, maxScore), assessment_tier_change (sessionId, fromTier, toTier, direction: 'up' | 'down'), assessment_completed (sessionId, totalScore, cefrLevel), onboarding_step_started (stepId, stepType), onboarding_step_completed (stepId), onboarding_completed (definitionId)
Events are properly typed as members of the discriminated union (each with unique type field)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GameEventBus.ts uses a discriminated union type GameEvent with a 'type' field. Add the new types to this union following the same pattern as existing events.

userStories.title: Assessment EVAL block parser and system prompt builder
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a parser for the **ASSESSMENT_EVAL** structured block and a system prompt builder for the assessment conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/assessment/assessment-prompt-utils.ts with:

buildAssessmentSystemPrompt(basePrompt, tierDefinitions, currentTier, targetLanguage) — extends the base NPC system prompt with: tier escalation rules, ASSESSMENT_EVAL block format instructions, adaptive behavior rules (rephrase if struggling, advance if easy)

parseAssessmentEvalBlock(response: string): { scores: {oralFluency, pronunciation, grammarAccuracy, vocabularyRange, pragmaticCompetence} | null, cleanedResponse: string } — extracts dimension scores from **ASSESSMENT_EVAL** ... **END_ASSESSMENT_EVAL** block using regex, same pattern as parseGrammarFeedbackBlock() in shared/language/progress.ts

The ASSESSMENT_EVAL format: **ASSESSMENT_EVAL** OralFluency: [1-5] Pronunciation: [1-5] GrammarAccuracy: [1-5] VocabularyRange: [1-5] PragmaticCompetence: [1-5] Tier: [1-3] Struggling: [true/false] **END_ASSESSMENT_EVAL**
Export both functions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact same regex-based parsing pattern used by parseGrammarFeedbackBlock() in shared/language/progress.ts lines 186-226. The prompt builder should instruct the LLM to always include this block after its conversational response.

userStories.title: Assessment conversation controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a controller for the conversational assessment phase that manages tier escalation, automated metrics, and transcript storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/assessment/AssessmentConversationController.ts
Constructor takes: tierDefinitions (from ARRIVAL_ENCOUNTER Phase 1), targetLanguage, gameEventBus, callbacks for sendMessage/onResponse
Tier tracking: starts at Tier 1, advances after 3+ successful exchanges (scores avg >= 3), drops back if struggling (scores avg < 2). Emits assessment_tier_change events
Timer: 8-10 min window, fires graceful wrap-up callback when 1 min remaining

Automated metrics tracked in real-time: speechRateWPM (words/time), lexicalDiversityTTR (unique words/total words), meanLengthOfUtterance (avg words per turn), responseLatencyMs (time between NPC done and player response), repairRequests (count 'repeat'/'again'/'what?' patterns), codeSwitchingInstances (count of English words when target != English)
Stores full transcript: array of {role: 'player' | 'npc', text: string, timestamp: number}
Uses parseAssessmentEvalBlock from US-014 on each NPC response to collect dimension scores
getResults() returns: average dimension scores, automated metrics, highest sustained tier, transcript as RecordingReference
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This controller wraps around the existing chat flow — it doesn't directly use BabylonChatPanel but accepts callbacks for message I/O. The AssessmentEngine (US-016) will wire it to the actual chat panel.

userStories.title: Assessment engine state machine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a client-side assessment engine that manages the full assessment lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/assessment/AssessmentEngine.ts
State machine with states: 'idle' | 'initializing' | 'phase_active' | 'phase_transitioning' | 'scoring' | 'complete'
Constructor accepts: AssessmentDefinition, GameEventBus, callbacks object (for server API calls, NPC spawning, UI updates)
start() — sets state to initializing, emits assessment_started event, transitions to first phase
advancePhase() — stores current phase result, transitions to next phase or scoring if last phase. Emits phase events
submitPhaseResult(phaseId, result: PhaseResult) — stores result, calls server persist callback
complete() — computes total score (sum of phase scores), calls mapScoreToCEFR(), emits assessment_completed, calls server complete callback. Sets state to 'complete'
getState() returns: current state, active phase index, running scores, completed phases
Manages RecordingReference array — each phase handler adds recordings via addRecording(ref)
Engine does not depend on Babylon.js directly — uses callbacks for all UI/game integration
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The engine orchestrates but does not implement phase-specific logic. Phase handlers (conversation controller, listening task, etc.) are separate and communicate through callbacks.

userStories.title: Assessment listening task handler (Phase 2)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need handlers for listening comprehension: Task 2A (following directions) and Task 2B (information extraction).
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/assessment/AssessmentListeningTask.ts

Task 2A handler: accepts multi-step directions config, provides callback-based API for reporting step completion (correct destination 1pt, steps followed 0-3pts, asked for repetition noted but not penalized). Returns TaskResult with score /4
Task 2B handler: accepts 3-option descriptions config, provides selection UI data (3 questions: simple detail 1pt, comparative 1pt, inferential 1pt). Returns TaskResult with score /3
Both tasks store NPC prompt text as transcript in RecordingReference
Combined phase score: /7
Emits assessment_phase_completed event when both tasks done
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Task 2A uses CharacterController position tracking — accepts a callback to check player position against waypoints. Task 2B presents choices and records selections. Neither task depends on Babylon.js directly.

userStories.title: Assessment writing task handler (Phase 3)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need handlers for the writing phase: Task 3A (form completion) and Task 3B (brief message).
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/assessment/AssessmentWritingTask.ts

Task 3A handler: defines form fields (name-not-scored, nationality 1pt, reason 1pt, lengthOfStay 1pt, specialRequests 2pt), accepts submitted text for each field, calls LLM scoring callback to evaluate comprehensibility/correctness. Returns TaskResult with score /5

Task 3B handler: provides contextual writing prompt, accepts 2-4 sentence response, calls LLM scoring callback evaluating taskCompletion (0-2), vocabulary (0-2), grammar (0-2). Returns TaskResult with score /6
All player text stored verbatim as transcript in RecordingReference
Combined phase score: /11
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: LLM scoring is done via a callback — the handler provides the text and scoring rubric, the caller sends to the LLM API. This keeps the handler independent of the specific LLM implementation.

userStories.title: Assessment visual recognition task handler (Phase 4)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need handlers for visual recognition: Task 4A (sign reading) and Task 4B (object identification).
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/assessment/AssessmentVisualTask.ts

Task 4A handler: accepts array of 5 sign definitions (each: targetLanguageText, correctMeaning, distractors[2]), provides selection data for UI, scores selections at 1pt each. Returns TaskResult with score /5

Task 4B handler: accepts array of 5 object definitions (each: targetLanguageName, correctEnglish, distractors[2]), supports both verbal response (calls scorePronunciation from shared/language/pronunciation-scoring.ts with threshold >= 60 for correct) and selection fallback. Returns TaskResult with score /5
Signs and objects are configurable per target language — definitions passed in, not hardcoded
Combined phase score: /10
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sign/object data will come from the AssessmentDefinition config. The handler is UI-agnostic — it provides data structures and accepts responses via callbacks.

userStories.title: Assessment progress UI overlay
userStories.description: As a player, I want subtle progress indicators during the assessment showing which phase I'm in and time remaining.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/assessment/AssessmentProgressUI.ts using @babylonjs/gui
Phase progress indicator: 4 dots/circles showing phases 1-4, active phase highlighted, completed phases filled
Timer display for timed phases (conversation 10min, writing 4min) — countdown format MM:SS, turns amber at 2min, red at 1min
Phase transition overlay: brief fullscreen-fade text (e.g. 'Let us head to the hotel...' or 'One more thing before you go...') lasting 2-3 seconds
show()/hide()/updatePhase(index)/updateTimer(seconds)/showTransition(text) API
Uses same visual style as BabylonQuestTracker.ts (similar positioning, colors, fonts)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Keep the UI minimal and non-intrusive. The phase dots should be in a corner, timer near the top. Transition text should be centered with a semi-transparent background.

userStories.title: Assessment results summary panel
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my assessment results at completion showing my CEFR level and dimension scores.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/assessment/AssessmentResultsPanel.ts using @babylonjs/gui
Overall score display: percentage and raw score (e.g. '72% — 38/53')
CEFR level badge: large display with level (e.g. 'B1') and description text from getCEFRDescription()
5-dimension score display: horizontal bars for Oral Fluency, Pronunciation, Grammar Accuracy, Vocabulary Range, Pragmatic Competence — each scaled 1-5, color-coded (red/yellow/green)
Call-to-action button: 'Your adventure begins!' that closes the panel
show(assessmentSession: AssessmentSession)/hide() API
Uses same visual style as BabylonSkillTreePanel.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the panel shown once at assessment completion. The RPG progress panel (US-028) is the persistent version accessible from the menu. This one is a one-time congratulatory display.

userStories.title: Onboarding manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an onboarding manager that orchestrates the step-by-step onboarding sequence, interleaving tutorial steps with assessment phases.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/onboarding/OnboardingManager.ts
Constructor accepts: OnboardingDefinition, AssessmentEngine reference, GameEventBus, callbacks for UI actions (showTutorialOverlay, highlightUIElement, triggerNPCDialogue)
start() — initializes OnboardingState, begins step 0, emits onboarding_step_started

advanceStep() — marks current step completed, progresses to next. Dispatches by step type: narrative (calls triggerNPCDialogue callback), movement_tutorial/interaction_tutorial/ui_tutorial (calls showTutorialOverlay with config), assessment_phase (calls assessmentEngine to run corresponding phase)
skipStep() — marks optional steps as skipped, advances to next
complete() — emits onboarding_completed, calls completion callback with final OnboardingState
getState() returns current OnboardingState
Persists state via callback (caller saves to playthrough saveData)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The manager orchestrates but does not implement UI or game logic. All effects happen through callbacks. This keeps it testable and decoupled from Babylon.js.

userStories.title: BabylonGame integration — first playthrough detection and onboarding launch
userStories.description: As a player starting a new game, I want to be automatically guided through the onboarding/assessment sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify BabylonGame.ts init() method: after loadNPCs() and before startGameLoop(), check playthrough saveData for onboardingComplete flag
If first playthrough (no onboardingComplete): import ARRIVAL_ENCOUNTER and LANGUAGE_LEARNING_ONBOARDING, create AssessmentEngine and OnboardingManager, call onboardingManager.start()
Wire OnboardingManager callbacks: triggerNPCDialogue -> use existing BabylonChatPanel, showTutorialOverlay -> show/hide AssessmentProgressUI, assessment phase -> use AssessmentEngine
On onboarding completion: store { onboardingComplete: true, assessmentSessionId, cefrLevel } in playthrough saveData via existing save mechanism
Set initial effectiveFluency in LanguageProgressTracker based on CEFR level (A1->10, A2->25, B1->45, B1+->65, B2->80)
Guide NPC: designate an existing NPC near spawn as the guide (use first loaded NPC or spawn a special guide character)
If not first playthrough: skip directly to startGameLoop()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BabylonGame.ts is in client/src/components/3DGame/. The init() method handles the full game startup sequence. Insert the onboarding check at the right point in the sequence. Use dynamic imports for assessment modules to avoid bloating the main bundle.

userStories.title: Periodic assessment definition and level-up trigger
userStories.description: As a player leveling up at tier boundaries, I want to be reassessed so the game tracks my improvement.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared/assessment/periodic-encounter.ts exporting PERIODIC_ENCOUNTER: AssessmentDefinition — abbreviated assessment with only Phase 1 (conversational, 5 min, maxScore 25, same 5 dimensions, faster tier escalation: advance after 2 exchanges instead of 3)
Modify client/src/components/3DGame/LanguageGamificationTracker.ts: add callback hook onTierBoundaryLevelUp that fires at levels 5, 10, 15, 20
Add minimum gameplay time check: only trigger if > 60 minutes since last assessment (track lastAssessmentTimestamp in gamification state)
When triggered: emit event that BabylonGame can listen for to start abbreviated assessment using AssessmentEngine with PERIODIC_ENCOUNTER definition
Store result with assessmentType 'periodic'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The periodic encounter is conversational-only, shorter (5 min vs 10 min), and uses faster tier escalation. The gamification tracker detects the level-up but does not run the assessment itself — it fires an event.

userStories.title: Integrate assessment CEFR with existing systems
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the assessment CEFR level to feed into difficulty adaptation, content gating, and NPC prompt generation.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify buildPlayerProficiencySection() in shared/language/utils.ts (moved from shared/language-utils.ts in US-002): add optional cefrLevel parameter. When provided, use CEFR-derived fluency ranges instead of raw overallFluency for tier selection (A1->0-20, A2->20-40, B1->40-60, B1+->60-80, B2->80-100)

Modify client/src/components/3DGame/ContentGatingManager.ts: add cefrLevel as gating criterion — A1 gates nothing extra, A2 unlocks intermediate NPCs, B1 unlocks advanced quests, B2 unlocks all content
Store Phase 1 dimension scores in LanguageProgress metadata so NPC prompts can reference specific weak dimensions (e.g. 'player is weak in pronunciation' -> NPCs focus on clear speech)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This wires the assessment results into the existing adaptive difficulty system. The changes to buildPlayerProficiencySection and ContentGatingManager should be minimal — adding a parameter and an additional check.

userStories.title: Admin assessment dashboard
userStories.description: As an admin/researcher, I want a dashboard showing system-wide assessment data with drill-down capability.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/analytics/AssessmentDashboard.tsx React component
Fetches data from GET /api/assessments/world/:worldId/summary

Displays: score distribution histogram (simple bar chart using div elements, no chart library required), CEFR level distribution (colored count badges), per-phase score averages table, automated metrics averages table (WPM, TTR, MLU, latency, grammar error rate, code-switching)
Filter controls: world selector dropdown, assessment type radio (all/pre/post/periodic), date range inputs
Summary stat cards at top: total assessments count, mean score, mean improvement (pre-to-post delta)
Player list table with columns: name, assessment count, latest CEFR, latest score — each row clickable
Follow same styling patterns as existing ResearcherDashboard.tsx
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use simple HTML/CSS for charts (div-based bars) to avoid adding chart library dependencies. Follow the same component patterns as ResearcherDashboard.tsx for layout, card styling, and table formatting.

userStories.title: Player assessment detail view (admin)
userStories.description: As an admin/researcher, I want to drill down into an individual player's assessment history.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/analytics/PlayerAssessmentDetail.tsx React component
Fetches data from GET /api/assessments/player/:playerId
Longitudinal score display: table or simple line chart showing assessment scores over time (pre -> periodic -> post)
Per-dimension display: 5 horizontal bars for Phase 1 dimensions for each assessment, side-by-side comparison if multiple assessments
Phase breakdown table: expandable rows showing phase scores and task-level details
Automated metrics comparison: table with columns for each assessment showing WPM, TTR, MLU, latency, grammar error rate with delta indicators
Recordings section: list of recording references with links (transcript text displayed inline, audio URL as download link)
Back button to return to AssessmentDashboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Accessible from AssessmentDashboard by clicking a player row. Use URL params (/analytics/assessments/player/:playerId) for direct linking. Keep the UI simple — tables and colored bars, no complex charting library.

userStories.title: Player-facing RPG progress panel (in-game)
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my language assessment results as an RPG-style character sheet in-game.
acceptanceCriteria: Create client/src/components/3DGame/assessment/PlayerAssessmentPanel.ts using @babylonjs/gui

Language Stats section: 5 horizontal stat bars (Oral Fluency, Pronunciation, Grammar Accuracy, Vocabulary Range, Pragmatic Competence) each scaled 1-5, color-coded by value (1-2 red, 3 yellow, 4-5 green)
CEFR rank badge: centered display with level text and description
If multiple assessments exist: show improvement arrows (up/down/same Unicode arrows) next to each stat comparing latest to previous
Next assessment indicator: 'Next assessment at: Level X' based on next tier boundary
Accessible via hotkey (L key) or game menu — register with BabylonGUIManager panel system
show()/hide()/toggle() API, loads latest assessment data from saved state
Uses same visual patterns as BabylonSkillTreePanel.ts (background, borders, text styles)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the persistent in-game panel (accessible anytime), unlike the one-time results panel from US-021. Data comes from playthrough saveData where assessment results are stored.

userStories.title: Recording and transcript storage integration
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want speech recordings and conversation transcripts persisted for each assessment.
acceptanceCriteria: In AssessmentConversationController (US-015): ensure full transcript (all turns with timestamps) is stored as RecordingReference via AssessmentEngine.addRecording()
In AssessmentListeningTask (US-017): store NPC direction/description text as transcript RecordingReference
In AssessmentWritingTask (US-018): store all player text responses verbatim as transcript RecordingReference
In AssessmentEngine (US-016): on complete(), call server API PUT /api/assessments/:sessionId/recordings for each collected RecordingReference
Admin PlayerAssessmentDetail (US-027) already displays recordings — verify transcript text shows inline and audio URLs (if present) show as links
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story wires together the recording storage across all phase handlers. Audio recording is only available when STT is active — transcript text is always available. RecordingReference objects flow: phase handler -> AssessmentEngine -> server API -> MongoDB.
